title: Small-Cap ETF Showdown: Schwab's SCHA vs. iShares' IJR
author: Robert Izquierdo
description: The Schwab U.S. Small-Cap ETF (SCHA) and iShares Core S&P Small-Cap ETF (IJR) offer different approaches to small-cap investing. SCHA provides broader diversification with 1,721 holdings and a lower 0.04% expense ratio, delivering higher 1-year returns (44% vs 37.1%), but carries higher volatility. IJR offers a more selective portfolio of 640 quality-screened holdings with lower risk and higher liquidity ($102.9B AUM), making it suitable for risk-conscious investors willing to pay a slightly higher 0.06% expense ratio.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: SCHA demonstrates superior 1-year returns (44% vs 37.1%), lowest expense ratio (0.04%), and broader market representation with 1,721 holdings, making it attractive for growth-focused investors seeking comprehensive small-cap exposure.

IJR offers lower volatility, reduced maximum drawdown (28% vs 30.8%), substantial AUM ($102.9B), quality-focused screening, and higher dividend yield (1.2%), making it suitable for risk-averse investors prioritizing stability.
Mentioned as a top holding in SCHA with recent positive price movement (+17.03%), but included only as a portfolio component without independent analysis or recommendation.
Listed as the largest position in IJR (0.74%), mentioned factually without independent sentiment or performance assessment.

title: Why Viavi Solutions Stock Triumphed on Thursday
author: Eric Volkman
description: Viavi Solutions stock surged over 15% after beating Q3 earnings estimates with $407M in revenue (43% YoY growth) and exceeding guidance expectations. The company's strong performance is driven by thriving demand in data center and aerospace industries, with optimistic outlook for continued growth in both sectors.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company significantly beat Q3 earnings estimates ($407M vs $394M consensus) and Q4 guidance ($427-437M revenue vs $402M consensus). Operating income surged 79% YoY. Strong positioning in two high-growth industries (data center and aerospace) with positive forward outlook supports bullish outlook.

title: Viavi Stock Is Trending Overnight: Here's What You Should Know
author: Mohd Haider
description: Viavi Solutions (NASDAQ: VIAV) shares surged 25.17% after-hours following strong Q3 results that beat earnings estimates by 22.73% and revenue by 3.32%. The company reported net revenue of $406.8 million (up 42.8% YoY) and non-GAAP EPS of $0.27 (up 80% YoY), driven by growth in data center and aerospace/defense markets. Bullish Q4 guidance projecting revenue of $427-$437 million and EPS of $0.29-$0.31 further boosted investor confidence.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong Q3 earnings and revenue beats, significant YoY growth in net revenue (42.8%) and EPS (80%), expanded operating margins, bullish Q4 guidance, and substantial after-hours stock price increase (25.17%) demonstrate excellent financial performance and positive market reception.

title: The Iran Ceasefire Sent Tech Stocks Soaring. Here Are the 3 That Have the Most Room Left to Run.
author: Micah Zimmerman
description: Following the Iran ceasefire announcement in early April 2026, tech stocks rebounded sharply. The article identifies three AI infrastructure companies with structural momentum: Marvell Technology, which partners with Nvidia on custom XPUs and networking; Lumentum Holdings, which manufactures photonic components for AI data centers and is sold out through 2027; and Viavi Solutions, which provides testing and GPS-independent navigation solutions for AI infrastructure in disrupted environments.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Announced $2 billion strategic partnership with Nvidia on NVLink Fusion, building connective tissue for AI factories. Stock rallied ~20% on ceasefire news, with investors repricing the long-term revenue implications of the Nvidia endorsement.

Received $2 billion Nvidia investment with multibillion-dollar purchase commitment and new U.S. fabrication facility agreement. Company is completely sold out through end of 2027 with no end to demand in sight, providing clear visibility into next 24 months of AI spending.

Positioned at intersection of AI data center validation and GPS-independent defense/navigation technology. Recent maritime anti-jamming solution launch and 30+ demonstrations at MWC Barcelona 2026 align with D3SOE (Denied, Degraded, Disrupted) environments exposed by Iran conflict. Trades at lower multiple than peers, suggesting upside potential.
Strategic $2 billion investments in both Marvell and Lumentum demonstrate commitment to securing AI infrastructure supply chains and building next-generation data center capabilities.

title: Stock Market Today, Mar. 12: Ondas Jumps on Palantir Partnership
author: Emma Newbery
description: Ondas (ONDS) surged 5.24% on news of a strategic partnership with Palantir Technologies and World View to develop an AI-driven defense platform integrating drones, balloons, and ground robots. The partnership follows recent announcements of a merger with Mistral and acquisition of Bird Aerosystems, signaling Ondas' shift toward defense and security. The stock has gained 57.47% over the past six months, with Needham & Company maintaining a Buy rating.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock jumped 5.24% on announcement of major partnership with Palantir and World View. Company is executing strategic shift toward defense/security with multiple recent deals (Mistral merger, Bird Aerosystems acquisition). Analyst maintained Buy rating and stock has gained 57.47% in six months.
Partnership with Ondas expands Palantir's AI platform applications in defense sector. Stock closed up 1.25%, indicating positive market reception to the strategic collaboration.

Stock declined 16.39% despite being in the same communication equipment industry. Significant pullback after gaining 141.40% in the past month suggests profit-taking and potential overvaluation concerns.
Minor decline of 1.10% in communication equipment sector. Modest movement suggests no significant market reaction or company-specific news driving the stock.
